title: Weather Dashboard
subtitle: Read the Sky, Code the Data!
overview: In this session, students build a mini Weather Dashboard using sensors and Arduino. They learn how real-world weather stations measure temperature, humidity, and other parameters — and display live data just like a professional weather app. Students connect DHT11 sensors to Arduino, read data via code, and display it on an LCD or Serial Monitor.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — DHT11 sensor close-ups, LCD display outputs, student wiring activity, weather station diagrams, Serial Monitor screenshots.

keyTopics: What is a Weather Dashboard? — Displays real-time climate data
DHT11 Sensor — Measures Temperature (°C) and Humidity (%)
How Sensors Convert Physical Data to Digital Signals
Connecting DHT11 to Arduino — VCC, GND, DATA pins
Reading Sensor Data Using Arduino Code and DHT Library
Displaying Data on Serial Monitor vs LCD Display (16x2)
What is IoT? — Devices that sense, collect, and share data
Real-world Applications — Weather stations, smart homes, agriculture

activity.title: Build a Mini Weather Dashboard with DHT11 + Arduino
parts: USB cable and laptop with Arduino IDE
steps: Connect DHT11 sensor: VCC → 5V, GND → GND, DATA → Pin 2 on Arduino
Install the DHT sensor library in Arduino IDE (Sketch → Include Library → Manage Libraries)
Upload the basic DHT11 code and open Serial Monitor at 9600 baud
Observe temperature (°C) and humidity (%) values printing live
Connect 16x2 LCD via I2C module: VCC → 5V, GND → GND, SDA → A4, SCL → A5
Update code to display readings on LCD instead of Serial Monitor
Challenge: Add an IF condition — display '🌡️ Too Hot!' if temp > 35°C
Move sensor near a window, near a fan, and near a lamp — compare readings!
Groups present their dashboard and discuss: why do readings differ across the room?

quiz.q: What does the DHT11 sensor measure?
quiz.explanation: DHT11 is a temperature & humidity sensor! It measures how hot/cold and how moist the air is.

options: The amount of moisture/water vapour in the air
quiz.explanation: Humidity measures water vapour in the air. High humidity = feels sticky and moist. Low = dry air.

quiz.q: Which pin on the DHT11 sends data to the Arduino?
quiz.explanation: The DATA pin carries the sensor readings from DHT11 to the Arduino. VCC = power, GND = ground.

quiz.q: What is the Arduino Serial Monitor used for?
options: To display text and data output from the Arduino program
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor is a window in Arduino IDE that shows text output — perfect for viewing live sensor data!
LCD = Liquid Crystal Display! It uses liquid crystals to display characters, numbers, and symbols.

quiz.q: In a 16x2 LCD, what do the numbers 16 and 2 represent?
quiz.explanation: 16x2 means 16 characters across and 2 rows — enough to display temperature on row 1 and humidity on row 2!

options: A network of physical devices that collect and share data via the internet
A special type of computer virus
quiz.explanation: IoT = smart devices (sensors, appliances) that connect to the internet to share real-world data automatically.

quiz.q: A weather app on your phone gets data from:
options: Real sensors in weather stations that collect and send data
quiz.explanation: Weather apps use data from thousands of weather stations worldwide — each packed with sensors just like your DHT11!

quiz.q: What library must be installed in Arduino IDE to use the DHT11 sensor?
quiz.explanation: The DHT sensor library (by Adafruit) gives Arduino the functions needed to read data from the DHT11 sensor.

quiz.q: Temperature in the Arduino code is displayed in which unit by default with DHT11?
quiz.explanation: DHT11 returns temperature in Celsius (°C) by default. You can convert to Fahrenheit using: F = (C × 9/5) + 32.

quiz.q: If temperature reads 38°C, what would a smart dashboard display as a warning?
options: Too Hot! — using an IF condition in code
quiz.explanation: With IF conditions in Arduino code, you can trigger warnings: if (temp > 35) { display 'Too Hot!'; }

quiz.q: Why might sensor readings differ near a window vs near a fan?
options: Temperature and humidity vary in different parts of a room
quiz.explanation: Real environments vary! Sunlight heats the air near a window; fans cool it nearby. Sensors measure these real differences.

quiz.q: What does the VCC pin on the DHT11 connect to on the Arduino?
quiz.explanation: VCC = power supply pin. Connect it to Arduino's 5V pin to give the DHT11 the electricity it needs to work.

quiz.q: Which of these is a real-world application of weather sensor data?
options: Smart irrigation — watering crops only when humidity is low
Changing font sizes on a website
quiz.explanation: Smart farming uses humidity and temperature sensors to decide when to water crops — saving water and improving yields!

quiz.q: What is the main skill students practise by building the Weather Dashboard?
options: Connecting real-world sensors to code to display meaningful data
quiz.explanation: The Weather Dashboard project brings it all together — hardware wiring, sensor reading, coding logic, and data display. Real engineering!
